mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% in Dec 2009 suggests recovery phase beginning
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 5-trading-day window as market participants rapidly price in demand signals.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352985M in Dec 2009 shows post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS growth and 5d forward returns drops below 0.08 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at near-zero levels combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals sustained monetary accommodation, reducing real discount rates and supporting equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon as investors rotate into risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL at 217.541 in Dec 2009
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when both conditions are met versus when either is violated.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expansion signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, which supports profit margins and equity returns over 20 trading days as operational efficiency gains materialize.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.3043 in Dec 2009 shows post-recession rebound
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 56% of instances when INDPRO growth exceeds trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline signals weakening real estate demand and future consumption headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as market anticipates reduced household wealth and spending.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 574K in Nov 2009 remains depressed post-crisis
regime_hint: housing market weakness during deleveraging cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in fewer than 52% of instances when HOUST declines exceed trigger threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust labor market recovery and rising wage pressures, which drive positive equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings growth from higher consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE month-over-month decline > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130910K and UNRATE at 10.0% in Dec 2009
regime_hint: synchronized labor market recovery with employment gains
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20d forward returns when both conditions are met do not significantly exceed (t-test p > 0.05) returns when conditions are not jointly satisfied.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration signals strengthening economic activity and corporate profit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings estimates are revised upward in response to demand growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12973.0B in Q3 2009 shows early recovery
regime_hint: cyclical expansion phase with accelerating growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns in high-growth quarters are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) than in low-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate policy headwinds.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL growth minimal in Dec 2009
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20d forward returns when both conditions are met are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) than when conditions are not jointly satisfied.
